hikaricp-connection-url.name: hikaricp-connection-url
hikaricp-connection-url.description: A database connection URL used to connect to a database. May contain database system name, host, port, database name and some parameters. The exact syntax of a database connection URL is specified by your DBMS.

hikaricp-driver-classname.name: hikaricp-driver-classname
hikaricp-driver-classname.description: The fully-qualified class name of the JDBC driver. Example: com.mysql.jdbc.Driver

hikaricp-driver-locations.name: hikaricp-driver-locations
hikaricp-driver-locations.description: Comma-separated list of files/folders and/or URLs containing the driver JAR and its dependencies (if any). For example '/var/tmp/mariadb-java-client-1.1.7.jar'

hikaricp-kerberos-user-service.name: hikaricp-kerberos-user-service
hikaricp-kerberos-user-service.description: Specifies the Kerberos User Controller Service that should be used for authenticating with Kerberos

hikaricp-password.name: hikaricp-password
hikaricp-password.description: The password for the database user

hikaricp-max-wait-time.name: hikaricp-max-wait-time
hikaricp-max-wait-time.description: The maximum amount of time that the pool will wait (when there are no available connections)  for a connection to be returned before failing, or 0 <time units> to wait indefinitely.

hikaricp-max-total-conns.name: hikaricp-max-total-conns
hikaricp-max-total-conns.description: This property controls the maximum size that the pool is allowed to reach, including both idle and in-use connections. Basically this value will determine the maximum number of actual connections to the database backend. A reasonable value for this is best determined by your execution environment. When the pool reaches this size, and no idle connections are available, the service will block for up to connectionTimeout milliseconds before timing out.

hikaricp-validation-query.name: hikaricp-validation-query
hikaricp-validation-query.description: Validation Query used to validate connections before returning them. When connection is invalid, it gets dropped and new valid connection will be returned. NOTE: Using validation might have some performance penalty.

hikaricp-min-idle-conns.name: hikaricp-min-idle-conns
hikaricp-min-idle-conns.description: This property controls the minimum number of idle connections that HikariCP tries to maintain in the pool. If the idle connections dip below this value and total connections in the pool are less than 'Max Total Connections', HikariCP will make a best effort to add additional connections quickly and efficiently. It is recommended that this property to be set equal to 'Max Total Connections'.

hikaricp-max-conn-lifetime.name: hikaricp-max-conn-lifetime
hikaricp-max-conn-lifetime.description: The maximum lifetime of a connection. After this time is exceeded the connection will fail the next activation, passivation or validation test. A value of zero or less means the connection has an infinite lifetime.
